unit_title: Nested Lists & String Formatting | Reading Material
content:
# Nested Lists & String Formatting

A list as an item of another list.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/fc717d9a-253a-40eb-98a8-0e0e24c5ab95.png)


## Accessing Nested List

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/c59826f5-47ed-4fa2-b6ac-2e0bce955a51.png)

#### Code

```python
list_a = [5, "Six", [8, 6], 8.2]
print(list_a[2])
```

#### Output

```

[8, 6]
```

### Accessing Items of Nested List

*Example - 1*

#### Code

```python
list_a = [5, "Six", [8, 6], 8.2]
print(list_a[2][0])
```

#### Output

```

8
```

*Example - 2*

#### Code

```python
list_a = ["Five", "Six"]
print(list_a[0][1])
```


#### Output

```

i
```

# String Formatting

#### Code

```python
name = input()
age = int(input())
msg = ("Hi " + name + ". You are "+ str(age) + " years old.")
print(msg)
```

String formatting simplifies this concatenation.

It increases the readability of code and type conversion is not required.

## Add Placeholders

Add placeholders `{}` where the string needs to be formatted.

```python
msg = "Hi {}. You are {} years old."
msg.format(val_1, val_2,..)
```

Inserts values inside the string’s placeholder  `{}`

#### Code

```python
name = "Raju"
age = 10
msg = "Hi {}. You are {} years old."
print(msg.format(name, age))
```


#### Output

```

Hi Raju. You are 10 years old.
```

### Number of Placeholders

#### Code

```python
name = "Raju"
age = 10
msg = "Hi {}. You are {} years old {}."
print(msg.format(name, age))
```

#### Output

```

IndexError: Replacement index 2 out of range for positional args tuple
```

## Numbering Placeholders

Numbering placeholders, will fill values according to the position of arguments.

#### Code

```python
name = input()
age = int(input())
msg = "Hi {0}. You are {1} years old."
print(msg.format(name, age))
```

#### Input

```

Raju
10
```

#### Output

```

Hi Raju. You are 10 years old.
```


#### Code

```python
name = input()
age = int(input())
msg = "Hi {1}. You are {0} years old."
print(msg.format(name, age))
```

#### Input

```

Raju
10
```

#### Output

```

Hi 10. You are Raju years old.
```

## Naming Placeholder

Naming placeholders will fill values according to the keyword arguments.

  

#### Code

```python
name = input()
age = int(input())
msg = "Hi {name}. You are {age} years old."
print(msg.format(name=name, age=age))
```

#### Input

```

Raju
10
```

#### Output

```

Hi Raju. You are 10 years old.
```
